stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Galapagos tortoises are the largest tortoises in the world.
  • They live on seven islands in the Galapagos archipelago, Ecuador.
  • The archipelago has a total of nineteen islands.
stem: The student wants to emphasize how many islands in the Galapagos archipelago have Galapagos tortoises living on them. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Only seven of the nineteen islands in the Galapagos archipelago have Galapagos tortoises living on them.
The largest tortoises in the world are found in the Galapagos archipelago.
Galapagos tortoises, the world's largest tortoises, live on islands in the Galapagos archipelago, Ecuador.
There are nineteen islands in Ecuador's Galapagos archipelago.

rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. The sentence emphasizes the number of islands in the archipelago that have Galapagos tortoises living on them, noting that seven of the nineteen islands have tortoises.

Choice B is incorrect. The sentence explains that the archipelago contains the world's largest tortoises; it doesn't identify these tortoises as Galapagos tortoises or emphasize how many of the archipelago's islands the tortoises live on. Choice C is incorrect. While the sentence does explain that Galapagos tortoises live on the islands of the archipelago, it doesn't emphasize how many of those islands the tortoises live on. Choice D is incorrect. The sentence specifies the total number of islands in the archipelago; it doesn't emphasize how many of those islands have Galapagos tortoises.
